discussion_comments.comment_body:
This behaviour for `broker_read_url` / `broker_write_url` may be surprising, as I think it's less clear cut whether an explicitly defined `broker_url` should be used ahead of specific env vars for read/write URLs.

In this case, I think that env vars for read/write URLs are probably more specific than explicit setting for `broker_url`, so I set the priority accordingly. 

Happy to adjust if necessary

@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),

discussion_comments.comment_body: My client wants to use this before migrating to quorum queues so the default is set to classic.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
+        native_delayed_delivery_queue_type=Option(default='classic', type='string'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
> My client wants to use this before migrating to quorum queues so the default is set to classic.

I understand, but why does it make sense?
It makes sense to me to be default `quorum` because that’s the only flow it is used.
If not, you force the user to 1) be aware of this flag and 2) set it.
Instead, setting it to `quorum` gives an automatic behavior, which is why `classic` doesn’t make sense to me.

You may bring this up with your client to ask for clarifications, but we want to reach an agreement based on what’s best for the code.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
+        native_delayed_delivery_queue_type=Option(default='classic', type='string'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
In a busy production environment, using quorum queues for all the native delayed delivery queues may overload RabbitMQ.
In addition, my client wants to test this feature without using quorum queues at first.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
+        native_delayed_delivery_queue_type=Option(default='classic', type='string'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
> In a busy production environment, using quorum queues for all the native delayed delivery queues may overload RabbitMQ.

This is an important comment, please add this as a warning in the official documentation for this feature.

> In addition, my client wants to test this feature without using quorum queues at first.

As a whole, the feature should not be configured to match a single user acceptance tests use-case.
In addition, if the your client's acceptance tests pass, then the default `classic` would be irrelevant as well, as to use the feature you’ll have to manually enable it.

It makes sense the default a.k.a automatic behavior would match the requirements of the feature, and not the requirements of temporal acceptance tests. For your client's case, the flag should be explicitly set for the tests and your client can rely on the defaults after confirming all is good.

P.S
Also, take note that after we integrate these changes, we are going to officially announce it is ready. If your client needs to do some testing to confirm production environment validity, then please pause the work on the PR and give your client a custom fork/version for testing. The rest of the community will not assume the flag is `classic` because “someone” is testing the feature, when it’s already merged to `main` and released.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
This flag is useless. The native delayed delivery needs to be enabled automatically if we detect quorum queues. The user shouldn’t be responsible for enabling that.

This creates an error-prone design where the user must learn of this flag instead of getting the behavior out of the box. You also use `is_native_delayed_delivery` later, which is currently bugged, but after the fix will be enough when enabled if the transport is `amqp` AND we detect quorum queues `True` (there’s a function for that too).

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
That's what the warning is for. We need this feature flag so that this can be turned on manually when all those exchanges and queues are declared only when needed.
People may choose to leave this turned off for their own reasons.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
> People may choose to leave this turned off for their own reasons.

Give me one example that it is useful as `False`.
Any case anyone can benefit from it?

I argue that the `False` path is not useful for any use-case, which is why it should be `True`, or actually not even used and assumed `True` automatically if quorum queues are detected, per no use case at all of the `False` option.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ def __repr__(self):
         heartbeat=Option(120, type='int'),
         heartbeat_checkrate=Option(3.0, type='int'),
         login_method=Option(None, type='string'),
+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
You may want to keep the old, timer based solution when not using quorum queues. Hence, the sensible default is `False`.
You may also want to use native delayed delivery without quorum queues, in which case the flag should be set to `True`. That's exactly what my client wants to do first.
They want to test this feature without using quorum queues first and later on migrate to quorum queues.

discussion_comments.commented_code:

+        native_delayed_delivery=Option(False, type='bool'),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
Ok I understand, we can have this flag as a hard-override and set it to `False` by default, which matches the default of classic queues, so it would keep using the loop timers as the ETA mechanism.

Thank you for explaining!

discussion_comments.comment_body:
- setting this option to False does nothing more compared to previous versions
- we believe the default value should be True so we need to transition from False to True
- to transition there are two ways I see :
  - issue a warning stating the default will change in a future version
  - change the default value to True in this branch and state the change in the change log. Setting this option to True probably has a low impact for users so it is a reasonable way to do it.

Do you suggest we should follow the second way?
